title: pyosys does not include const-returning functions
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Compile yosys with `ENABLE_PYOSIS=1`. Run `python3`.

```py
from pyosys import libyosys as ys

dir(ys.Pass)
```

## Expected behavior

`getPort` should appear in the available functions. So should `connections` and `getParam`.

## Actual behavior

None of these appear in the available functions.

## Discussion

In fact, nothing that returns a const seems to be in any available function list (e.g. `SigSpec::at`), except maybe `const char*`.

This is apparently because in `WType` in `py_wrap_generator.py`, `str_def` ends up being passed in as `const`, and not an actual type spec.

Unfortunately, just adding `const` as a prefix in the list of prefixes in `WFunction.from_string` and `WType.from_string` doesn't work, as that results in compilation errors later on:

```
[  0%] Building kernel/python_wrappers.o
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:3883:19: error: ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::Selection YOSYS_PYTHON::Design::selection()’ cannot be overloaded with ‘YOSYS_PYTHON::Selection YOSYS_PYTHON::Design::selection()’
 3883 |   const Selection selection();
      |                   ^~~~~~~~~
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:3880:13: note: previous declaration ‘YOSYS_PYTHON::Selection YOSYS_PYTHON::Design::selection(
’
 3880 |   Selection selection();
      |             ^~~~~~~~~
```

That one makes sense because there are actually two versions of `selection()`. I don't know a way around this, except maybe to add special code in `py_wrap_generator` for that one particular function.

The next errors seem to have to do with returns of const references. I don't know enough about how Boost Python handles these to contribute any guesses as to a solution.

```
kernel/python_wrappers.cc: In member function ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::SigBit YOSYS_PYTHON::SigSpec::at(int, const YOSYS_PYTHON::SigBit*)’:
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:6886:46: error: conversion from ‘const Yosys::RTLIL::SigBit’ to non-scalar type ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::SigBit’ requested
 6886 |   const SigBit ret_ = this->get_cpp_obj()->at(offset, *defval->get_cpp_obj());
      |                       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

```
kernel/python_wrappers.cc: In member function ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::SigSpec YOSYS_PYTHON::Cell::getPort(YOSYS_PYTHON::IdString*)’:
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:7053:52: error: conversion from ‘const Yosys::RTLIL::SigSpec’ to non-scalar type ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::SigSpec’ requested
 7053 |   const SigSpec ret_ = this->get_cpp_obj()->getPort(*portname->get_cpp_obj());
      |                        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

```
kernel/python_wrappers.cc: In member function ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::Const YOSYS_PYTHON::Cell::getParam(YOSYS_PYTHON::IdString*)’:
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:7100:51: error: conversion from ‘const Yosys::RTLIL::Const’ to non-scalar type ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::Const’ requested
 7100 |   const Const ret_ = this->get_cpp_obj()->getParam(*paramname->get_cpp_obj());
      |                      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

```
kernel/python_wrappers.cc: At global scope:
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:11523:18: error: no declaration matches ‘const YOSYS_PYTHON::Selection YOSYS_PYTHON::Design::selection()’
11523 |  const Selection Design::selection()
      |                  ^~~~~~
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:11516:12: note: candidate is: ‘YOSYS_PYTHON::Selection YOSYS_PYTHON::Design::selection()’
11516 |  Selection Design::selection()
      |            ^~~~~~
kernel/python_wrappers.cc:3741:9: note: ‘struct YOSYS_PYTHON::Design’ defined here
 3741 |  struct Design
      |         ^~~~~~
```

(and so on)
